content: Buddhism and Hinduism have different interpretations of reincarnation. Buddhism teaches that there is no permanent self (anatta) that reincarnates, while Hinduism believes in a permanent soul (atman) that undergoes rebirth. Both perspectives offer distinct philosophical views, and whether one is "right" over the other depends on personal beliefs and interpretations.
